name: South Los Angeles CDFI Consortium
description_short: South Los Angeles CDFI Consortium is a coalition of community development financial institutions and non-profit organizations providing financial assistance and counseling to underserved residents.
description_long: South Los Angeles CDFI Consortium operates as a collaborative network of community-based organizations focused on expanding financial access in the South Los Angeles region. The consortium functions as a resource hub connecting residents to multiple financial service providers, credit counseling, workforce development, and economic opportunity programs. Rather than operating as a single entity, it serves as a directory and coordination point for member organizations including credit unions, community development lenders, housing counselors, and workforce training providers. The consortium's approach reflects the CDFI (Community Development Financial Institution) model, which prioritizes serving low-income and underserved populations with fair lending practices and supportive financial services. Members include organizations specializing in small business loans, consumer credit products, housing assistance, and employment training. The consortium model allows residents to access multiple services through a single point of contact, reducing barriers to financial inclusion. The contact page lists partner organizations with varying specializations, enabling cross-referrals and comprehensive support. However, the website provides limited detail about the consortium's own operations, governance, or direct services, focusing primarily on partner contact information. This structure suggests the consortium's value lies in coordination and network effects rather than direct service delivery. The organization aligns with non-profit free-help standards by connecting consumers to affordable alternatives to predatory lending and providing access to certified financial counseling.
address: 303 Loma Dr Suite #202, Los Angeles, CA 90017

company_info.headquarters: 303 Loma Dr Suite #202, Los Angeles, CA 90017

pros: Connects residents to multiple CDFI lenders offering below-market rates and flexible terms
Includes access to credit union services and payday loan alternatives under 36% APR
Partners with HUD-approved housing counselors and economic development organizations
Provides workforce development connections through partner organizations like LETC
Non-profit coalition model prioritizes community benefit over profit extraction
Free initial contact and consultation with member organizations
Covers comprehensive financial needs: loans, credit counseling, housing, and employment
cons: Website provides minimal information about the consortium's own governance or operations
No clear explanation of how to navigate between partner organizations or which to contact first
Partner organizations have varying service areas; not all may serve all South LA residents
Limited transparency about eligibility requirements or application processes
No published data on outcomes, approval rates, or customer satisfaction from consortium members
best_for: South LA residents seeking affordable credit alternatives to payday or title loans
Small business owners looking for community development financing with technical assistance
Homebuyers needing HUD-approved counseling and affordable mortgage options
Workers seeking job training and employment support combined with financial services
diagnosis: Best for South LA residents seeking fair-lending alternatives and comprehensive financial support through a trusted non-profit network. Primary caveat: the consortium functions as a referral hub rather than a direct service provider, requiring users to contact individual partners for specific services and eligibility details.
services: Community development lending for consumers and small businesses
Credit union membership and payday loan alternatives (PALs)
Affordable mortgage lending and homebuyer support
Small business loans and economic development financing
